mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive forward returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 7.0 percent signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending expectations and triggers negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations where UNRATE exceeds 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength that lifts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over 60 trading days with a p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates capacity utilization improvements that boost profit margins and generate positive market returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of post-recovery observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, liquidity constraints vanish and risk appetite surges, producing immediate positive equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after 2012.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates confirms structural labor market tightening that supports wage growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative mean returns over 60 days in more than 45% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts during a late-cycle expansion phase signal peak consumer leverage and impending demand contraction, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rising HOUST values coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of late-cycle observations, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the wrong direction.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index reflect immediate output contraction that reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations and causes negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.
